raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/XXXX, there were XXXX purchases for {$970.00} ; XXXX declined, the other went through successfully on my credit card. I called Citi that day and they told me they couldn't stop it and to call XXXX. I immediately reached out to XXXX the same day. 

The only way to contact XXXX was via online chat and they stated they couldn't stop the charges but would send it to the fraud department. I called Citi back and they said dispute within XXXX of posting. This was all on XX/XX/XXXX the day of the purchases. 

XXXX emailed me on XX/XX/XXXX acknowledging someone got into my account and they changed the security and now I have to talk to Citi about the money. I spoke with multiple people in XXXX who said you have XXXX  days. I made the dispute on XX/XX/XXXX, after the holiday rush. 
. 
About XXXX days after filing the dispute I get an answer that I did not respond to XXXX in XXXX timely fashion to request a refund. I have emails and pdfs of chats where I requested this. the case was closed. I called and had Citi reopen it in XXXX and supplied the emails and chat pdfs. 

Citi sent a letter stating it was received. Then XXXX days later I got a letter that said it was not received and that it was now closed. I called XXXX, still in XXXX, they reopened and told me they will review by XX/XX/XXXX. I received nothing on XX/XX/XXXX. 

I called on XX/XX/XXXX to see what happened and was told it was still being reviewed. On XX/XX/XXXX I get a letter stating they ran out of time to review ( XXXX  days ) and the decision was final, so I am responsible for the fraudulent charges. 

I spoke with XXXX people today, XXXX managers, and all said we ran out of time, there was nothing I could do. I should have disputed earlier ( even though I was in XXXX  days ) to give them more time. They had from XXXX to XXXX and did nothing with the documents. XXXX after rep stated, nothing says the documents were insufficient, XXXX  just didn't take the time to review it in a timely fashion.
